domain: Mande kora 21-string harp (West Africa Mandinka; 21 nylon strings in two parallel rows separated by notched bridge; scale degrees alternate zigzag between rows; thumb plays bass on one side while forefinger plays melody on the other; shared resonator)
specific_mechanism: Two parallel rows of strings with bridge-enforced separation; successive scale degrees alternate between rows (zigzag interleave). One hand plays bass-ostinato on one row; other hand plays melodic ornament on the other row. Comb-interleaved register allocation.
llm_application: KORA-COMB-HEAD: a multi-head attention modification where each head's input feature dimensions are partitioned into TWO sub-groups via FIXED EVEN-ODD INTERLEAVE (dim 0,2,4,… → sub-group A; dim 1,3,5,… → sub-group B). Each sub-group has its own Q,K,V projections; the two sub-groups attend INDEPENDENTLY in parallel; their outputs are CONCATENATED along the dim axis (preserving original ordering). Each sub-group sees a coarse (low-pass) version of the full feature spectrum. Differs from dual-path/two-branch attention (which split by contiguous band) by using COMB-INTERLEAVED INDEX (every-other) so each sub-group is a spectrally-aliased version of the whole.
novelty_claim: U-shape Transformer with Frequency-Band Aware Attention splits time/frequency BANDS (contiguous). DPT-FSNet alternates intra-band + inter-time transformers. DualFormer fuses local-MBConv + global partition-attention BRANCHES. Multi-Head Latent Attention (TransMLA) compresses heads via latent. KORA-COMB-HEAD differs by (a) WITHIN-HEAD dim partition (not between heads), (b) FIXED EVEN-ODD INTERLEAVE (not contiguous band), (c) TWO SUB-GROUPS share resonator (concat output not gated), (d) ALIASING-INDUCING coarse-spectrum view per sub-group.
